description: Functions to create and execute tasks.

comment.description:
Execute Goose tasks on this thread.

Will return after `max_tasks` have been executed, or if there are no more tasks present.

param_comments.database: The database object to execute tasks for
param_comments.max_tasks: The maximum amount of tasks to execute

comment.description:
Creates a task state that can be used with goose_execute_tasks_state to execute tasks until
`goose_finish_execution` is called on the state.

`goose_destroy_state` must be called on the result.

param_comments.database: The database object to create the task state for

comment.return_value: The task state that can be used with goose_execute_tasks_state.

comment.description:
Execute Goose tasks on this thread.

The thread will keep on executing tasks forever, until goose_finish_execution is called on the state.
Multiple threads can share the same goose_task_state.

param_comments.state: The task state of the executor

comment.description:
Execute Goose tasks on this thread.

The thread will keep on executing tasks until either goose_finish_execution is called on the state,
max_tasks tasks have been executed or there are no more tasks to be executed.

Multiple threads can share the same goose_task_state.

param_comments.state: The task state of the executor
param_comments.max_tasks: The maximum amount of tasks to execute

comment.return_value: The amount of tasks that have actually been executed

comment.description: Finish execution on a specific task.

param_comments.state: The task state to finish execution

comment.description: Check if the provided goose_task_state has finished execution
comment.return_value: Whether or not goose_finish_execution has been called on the task state

comment.description:
Destroys the task state returned from goose_create_task_state.

Note that this should not be called while there is an active goose_execute_tasks_state running
on the task state.

param_comments.state: The task state to clean up

comment.description: Returns true if the execution of the current query is finished.

param_comments.con: The connection on which to check
